agent_output: {"integration_assessment":{"component_interactions":[{"from":"HTTP ingestion endpoint","to":"internal event pipeline","status":"correct","issue":"Structured events flow correctly with request IDs propagated"},{"from":"event pipeline","to":"data warehouse writer","status":"broken","issue":"Writes are not idempotent. Combined with at-least-once delivery semantics and retry logic (3 retries with exponential backoff), duplicate records are GUARANTEED under normal operation — not just edge cases. Every lost ACK, every circuit breaker recovery, every retry produces a duplicate row in the warehouse. This corrupts aggregate analytics silently."},{"from":"event pipeline","to":"dead-letter queue","status":"broken","issue":"DLQ exists but has no consumer process, no alerting on queue depth, and no reprocessing mechanism. Failed events enter a black hole. This is silent data loss disguised as resilience."},{"from":"retry logic","to":"data warehouse writer","status":"risk","issue":"Exponential backoff retries interact with the non-idempotent writes to amplify duplicates. A single transient network partition causing 1,000 ACK losses at 10k events/sec could produce thousands of duplicate warehouse records in seconds."}],"contract_compliance":"Event schema and HTTP contract appear sound. The critical contract violation is at the data layer: the service promises event delivery but actually delivers event-delivery-plus-unbounded-duplicates. Any downstream consumer of this warehouse data inherits corrupted counts.","data_flow_integrity":"Compromised. The happy path works, but any disruption — network blip, warehouse latency spike, circuit breaker trip-and-recover — injects duplicates with no detection or correction mechanism. At 10k events/sec, even a 0.1% duplicate rate is 10 phantom events per second compounding forever."},"observability_review":{"logging":{"coverage":"adequate","gaps":["No logging when a duplicate event is written (because duplicates are undetectable without deduplication logic)","No logging or metrics on DLQ depth or age of oldest message"]},"metrics":{"coverage":"Strong for the happy path — latency histograms, p99 dashboards, and alerts are excellent. But critical blind spots exist.","gaps":["No metric for duplicate event rate (impossible without idempotency keys)","No DLQ depth metric or age-of-oldest-message metric","No metric tracking ratio of retried-vs-first-attempt writes to warehouse","No data completeness metric comparing ingested count vs warehouse row count"]},"tracing":{"coverage":"Good — OpenTelemetry distributed tracing with request ID propagation covers the request lifecycle.","gaps":["Traces likely do not follow events into the DLQ or show that a DLQ'd event was never reprocessed","No trace correlation between an original event and its retry-generated duplicate"]},"debugging_capability":"Strong for latency and throughput issues. Weak for data correctness issues. An on-call engineer at 3am would quickly find a slow warehouse, but would have no way to detect or quantify duplicate records or silently lost DLQ events."},"resilience_assessment":{"failure_modes":[{"scenario":"Transient warehouse unavailability (seconds)","handling":"partial","recommendation":"Circuit breaker and retries handle availability correctly, but each retry can create a duplicate record. Add an idempotency key (event_id + timestamp hash) to warehouse writes using INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING or equivalent upsert."},{"scenario":"ACK lost after successful warehouse write","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"This is the most common duplicate source under at-least-once delivery. The fix is the same: idempotent writes keyed on a unique event identifier generated at the ingestion layer before any writes occur."},{"scenario":"DLQ accumulation beyond capacity","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"Deploy a DLQ consumer that retries with backoff, alerts on queue depth > threshold (e.g., 1000 messages or oldest message > 5 minutes), and routes to a permanent failure store after N attempts."},{"scenario":"Sustained warehouse outage (minutes to hours)","handling":"partial","recommendation":"Circuit breaker prevents cascade, events flow to DLQ. But since DLQ has no consumer, ALL events during the outage are permanently lost. This turns a temporary outage into permanent data loss."},{"scenario":"Burst beyond 10k events/sec","handling":"partial","recommendation":"Load tested at 10k/sec sustained, but no documented behavior for 2-5x bursts. Consider backpressure signaling (HTTP 429 with Retry-After) to prevent upstream from overwhelming the pipeline."}],"retry_config":"Well-configured: 3 retries with exponential backoff. The problem is not the retry mechanism itself but that retried writes are not idempotent.","timeout_config":"Not explicitly documented but implied by circuit breaker configuration. Verify warehouse write timeout is set below circuit breaker trip threshold.","circuit_breakers":"Present and appropriate for warehouse writes. Confirm half-open state probe frequency is tuned to warehouse recovery time.","graceful_degradation":"Partial. Circuit breaker prevents cascade failures, but degraded mode (DLQ routing) is a dead end since the DLQ has no consumer. Graceful degradation requires a path back to normal processing."},"operational_readiness":{"deployment_ready":false,"rollback_capable":true,"rollback_plan":"Blue/green deployment with load balancer traffic switch provides instant rollback. This is well-designed and production-grade.","monitoring_coverage":"Strong for availability and latency. No coverage for data correctness (duplicates, completeness, DLQ health). An on-call engineer cannot distinguish a healthy system from one silently producing thousands of duplicate records per minute.","on_call_documentation":"Needs runbooks for: (1) DLQ backup — how to drain and reprocess, (2) duplicate detection — how to identify and remediate duplicate warehouse records, (3) circuit breaker tripped — what to check and when to force-reset.","new_infrastructure_required":{"new_env_vars":[],"new_services":["DLQ consumer/reprocessor service"],"new_queues_topics":["Permanent failure queue (for events that fail DLQ reprocessing after N attempts)"],"config_changes":["Idempotency key column and unique constraint on warehouse target table","DLQ depth alerting threshold configuration"],"infra_provisioning":["Alerting rules for DLQ depth and age metrics"]},"database_safety":{"has_migrations":true,"reversible":true,"data_preserving":false,"large_table_impact":true,"details":"Adding a unique constraint for idempotency on a high-volume clickstream table requires careful migration. At 10k events/sec the table grows fast — adding a unique index on a large table can lock writes. Use CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY (Postgres) or equivalent non-blocking DDL for your warehouse. The current schema without idempotency is actively data-corrupting, not just suboptimal."},"verdict":"NOT READY"},"escalations":[{"type":"infrastructure_gap","issue":"Non-idempotent writes with at-least-once delivery guarantees duplicate data corruption","reason":"This is not a theoretical risk. At-least-once delivery plus non-idempotent writes equals guaranteed duplicates under normal operation (lost ACKs, retries after circuit breaker recovery). At 10k events/sec, duplicates compound rapidly and silently corrupt all downstream analytics. Requires schema change (idempotency key + unique constraint) and write logic change (upsert) before production traffic.","routed_to":"Coach K"},{"type":"infrastructure_gap","issue":"Dead-letter queue has no consumer — failed events are silently and permanently lost","reason":"The DLQ is the system's only safety net for failed writes, but nothing reads from it. During any sustained warehouse outage, every event is routed to the DLQ and never recovered. This converts temporary outages into permanent data loss. A DLQ consumer service must be deployed and DLQ depth alerting must be configured.","routed_to":"Coach K"},{"type":"missing_observability","issue":"No metrics or alerting for data correctness — duplicates and data loss are invisible","reason":"Current observability covers latency and throughput but has zero coverage for data integrity. An on-call engineer cannot detect duplicates or DLQ accumulation. Need: DLQ depth/age metrics with alerts, duplicate rate metric (post-idempotency-key), and ingested-vs-stored completeness ratio.","routed_to":"Coach K"}],"confidence":{"level":92,"high_confidence_areas":["Duplicate write risk is certain given at-least-once + non-idempotent writes","DLQ without consumer is confirmed silent data loss","Observability gaps for data correctness are clear from the described setup","Deployment and rollback strategy is solid"],"low_confidence_areas":["Exact warehouse technology and DDL migration strategy — recommendations are generic","Backpressure behavior beyond 10k/sec load test ceiling","Specific circuit breaker configuration parameters (trip threshold, half-open probe interval)"],"assumptions":["The warehouse supports upsert or ON CONFLICT semantics for idempotent writes","Event IDs or deterministic idempotency keys can be generated at the ingestion layer","The DLQ is a managed queue service (SQS, Pub/Sub, etc.) that supports depth monitoring"]}}
agent_output_excerpt: {"integration_assessment":{"component_interactions":[{"from":"HTTP ingestion endpoint","to":"internal event pipeline","status":"correct","issue":"Structured events flow correctly with request IDs propagated"},{"from":"event pipeline","to":"data warehouse writer","status":"broken","issue":"Writes are not idempotent. Combined with at-least-once delivery semantics and retry logic (3 retries with exponential backoff), duplicate records are GUARANTEED under normal operation — not just edge cases. Eve

content.thinking:
Let me assess this real-time analytics ingestion service based on the signals and gaps provided.

Key issues:
1. Non-idempotent writes + at-least-once delivery = guaranteed data corruption over time
2. Dead-letter queue with no consumer = silent data loss
3. No deduplication = duplicates compound under load/failures

This is NOT ready for production.

Every lost ACK, every circuit breaker recovery, every retry produces a duplicate row in the warehouse. This corrupts aggregate analytics silently."},{"from":"event pipeline","to":"dead-letter queue","status":"broken","issue":"DLQ exists but has no consumer process, no alerting on queue depth, and no reprocessing mechanism.
